patient_complaint: I have been having chest pain for the last 30 minutes. It feels like an elephant is sitting on my chest.

conversation_turns.message: Can you describe the pain? Is it sharp, dull, or pressure-like?
It's a heavy pressure. It goes down my left arm. I'm sweating a lot.
Have you had any heart problems before? Are you taking any medications?
I take blood pressure pills. My dad had a heart attack at 55. I'm 61.
We're going to get you back to a room right away. I'm putting you on a monitor now.

expected_disposition: immediate cardiac workup, 12-lead ECG within 10 minutes, cardiology consult
clinical_notes: 61yo M presenting with acute substernal chest pressure radiating to L arm with diaphoresis. Known HTN, FHx of premature CAD. STEMI alert criteria met.

patient_complaint: My 2-year-old daughter swallowed a button battery about an hour ago.

conversation_turns.message: Do you know what size the battery was? Is she having any symptoms?
It was from a remote control, one of those round flat ones. She seems okay but she's drooling more than usual.
Is she having any difficulty breathing or swallowing?
She keeps pointing at her throat but she's breathing fine. She won't drink her juice.
This is urgent. We need to get an X-ray immediately to locate the battery. I'm bringing you back now.

expected_disposition: emergent imaging, pediatric GI consult, likely emergent endoscopic removal
clinical_notes: 2yo F button battery ingestion approximately 1 hour ago. Drooling and refusal to drink suggest esophageal lodgment. High risk for caustic injury. Time-critical removal needed.

patient_complaint: I've been having a really bad headache for three days. Over-the-counter medicine isn't helping.

conversation_turns.message: Can you rate the headache on a scale of 1-10? Where exactly is the pain?
About a 6. It's on the right side of my head and behind my eye. It throbs.
Any fever, neck stiffness, vision changes, or vomiting?
No fever. Light bothers me a lot. I've felt nauseated but haven't thrown up.
Have you ever had headaches like this before?
I get migraines sometimes but this one just won't go away.

expected_disposition: urgent care evaluation, migraine workup, possible IV medication for status migrainosus
clinical_notes: 32yo F with known migraine history presenting with 3-day intractable right-sided headache with photophobia and nausea. No red flag symptoms. Likely status migrainosus requiring rescue therapy.

patient_complaint: I think I broke my ankle. I was playing basketball and rolled it badly. I can't put weight on it.

conversation_turns.message: When did this happen? Can you move your toes?
About 2 hours ago. Yeah, I can wiggle my toes but there's a lot of swelling on the outside of my ankle.
Is there any numbness or tingling in your foot? What color is the skin?
No numbness. The skin looks normal, just really swollen and bruised already.
Is the bone pushing against the skin or does the ankle look deformed?
No, it's just really swollen. It doesn't look crooked or anything.

expected_disposition: X-ray, likely lateral malleolus fracture vs severe sprain, splinting, orthopedic follow-up
clinical_notes: 24yo M with acute lateral ankle injury during basketball. NVI distally, no gross deformity. Meets Ottawa ankle rules criteria for imaging. Low acuity, stable.

patient_complaint: I can't see out of my right eye. It happened suddenly about 20 minutes ago.

conversation_turns.message: Is the vision completely gone or partially blocked? Any pain?
It's like a curtain came down from the top. No pain at all. I can still see a little at the bottom.
Have you had any head injury, flashing lights, or floaters recently?
I've been seeing flashing lights in that eye for the past week but I thought it was nothing.
Any history of diabetes or eye problems?
I'm nearsighted, pretty severely. I'm 57.

expected_disposition: emergent ophthalmology consult, likely retinal detachment requiring surgical repair within hours
clinical_notes: 57yo F with acute painless monocular vision loss OD, curtain-like field defect, 1-week prodrome of photopsias. High myopia is risk factor. Presentation consistent with rhegmatogenous retinal detachment. Time-sensitive.

patient_complaint: My husband is acting really confused and his speech doesn't make sense. This started about an hour ago.

conversation_turns.message: Was he normal before this started? Any weakness on one side?
He was fine at dinner. Then he started saying words that don't go together. His right hand keeps dropping things.
Is he having any trouble walking? Any headache or vomiting?
He's stumbling when he tries to walk. He doesn't seem to understand what I'm saying to him.
What time exactly did you first notice the changes? This is very important.
It was 6:45 PM. I remember because Jeopardy was starting and he couldn't answer any questions.

expected_disposition: stroke alert activation, immediate CT head, CTA, neurology consult, tPA evaluation if within window
clinical_notes: 68yo M with acute onset Wernicke aphasia, right-sided weakness and ataxia. Last known well 6:45 PM. Within tPA window. Code stroke activated.

patient_complaint: I've had a cough for about two weeks and now I'm coughing up some blood-tinged mucus.

conversation_turns.message: How much blood are we talking about? Streaks in the mucus or larger amounts?
Just streaks in the phlegm. Maybe a teaspoon total today. The phlegm is yellowish-green.
Any fever, shortness of breath, chest pain, or weight loss?
I've had a low-grade fever on and off. A little short of breath when I climb stairs. No weight loss.
Do you smoke? Any recent travel?
I quit smoking 5 years ago. I smoked for 20 years before that. No travel.

expected_disposition: chest X-ray, CBC, sputum culture, possible CT chest given smoking history, pulmonology referral if imaging abnormal
clinical_notes: 47yo M ex-smoker (20 pack-years) with 2-week productive cough, hemoptysis (small volume), low-grade fever. Likely infectious but smoking history warrants imaging to exclude malignancy.

patient_complaint: I've been having terrible stomach pain and diarrhea for two days. I can't keep anything down.

conversation_turns.message: Any blood in the diarrhea or vomit? When was your last meal?
No blood. I ate some leftover chicken two days ago and it started a few hours after that. I haven't been able to eat since.
Are you able to keep down small sips of water? How many times have you had diarrhea today?
Water comes back up too. I've been to the bathroom maybe 8 times today. I feel dizzy when I stand.
Any fever? Do you take any medications or have any medical conditions?
My temperature at home was 100.8. I take metformin for diabetes.

expected_disposition: IV fluid resuscitation, electrolyte panel, hold metformin, antiemetic, monitor for dehydration
clinical_notes: 38yo F with acute gastroenteritis likely foodborne, 48 hours duration with persistent vomiting and high-volume diarrhea. Orthostatic symptoms suggest significant dehydration. Diabetic on metformin - lactic acidosis risk with dehydration.

patient_complaint: I sliced my hand on a broken glass while washing dishes. It's bleeding a lot and I can see something white in the wound.

conversation_turns.message: Which hand and where exactly? Are you able to move all your fingers?
Left palm, near the base of my index finger. I can move my fingers but the index finger feels numb at the tip.
Are you applying pressure? Is the bleeding controlled?
I have a towel on it. It soaks through every few minutes. The cut is about 2 inches long.
Any allergies to anesthetics? When was your last tetanus shot?
No allergies. I think my last tetanus was maybe 12 years ago.

expected_disposition: laceration repair with exploration for tendon and digital nerve injury, tetanus booster, possible hand surgery consult for digital nerve repair
clinical_notes: 29yo M with deep laceration to L palm at base of index finger from broken glass. Visible tendon sheath, digital nerve deficit (numbness index fingertip). Hemostasis with pressure. Needs exploration under regional block. Tetanus update needed.

patient_complaint: I've been feeling really down for weeks and today I started thinking about ending my life.

conversation_turns.message: Thank you for telling me that. You're safe here. Do you have a plan to hurt yourself?
I have pills at home. I thought about taking all of them. I didn't, but I keep thinking about it.
Do you have the pills with you now? Have you taken anything today?
No, they're at home. I haven't taken anything extra. My friend drove me here because I told her what I was thinking.
That took a lot of courage. I'm going to get you into a safe room and have our crisis team come speak with you right away.
Okay. I just want the pain to stop. I lost my job three months ago and everything has fallen apart.

expected_disposition: psychiatric evaluation, 1:1 observation, safety screening, crisis intervention, possible voluntary admission
clinical_notes: 33yo F presenting with active suicidal ideation with plan (medication overdose) and access to means. Precipitating stressor: job loss 3 months ago. Protective factor: social support (friend brought her in), help-seeking behavior. No attempt made. Requires immediate psychiatric assessment and safety protocol.
